# DINOForge — Comprehensive LLM Reference

> A general-purpose mod platform and agent-driven development scaffold for Diplomacy is Not an Option.

DINOForge is a **mod operating system**, not a single mod. It provides a framework, registries, schemas, and tooling for building any type of mod — from simple balance tweaks to full total conversion packs.

**Tech Stack**: C# 12+ (.NET 8.0 / netstandard2.0), YAML/JSON, BepInEx 5.4.x, Unity ECS (DOTS), Addressables v1.21.18

**Design Philosophy**: Wrap proven libraries, never handroll. Declarative-first (YAML), agent-driven, contract-driven (schemas), observable (logs, overlays, validators).

**Status**: v0.5.0 (GUI installer, NuGet, stat modifiers, domain plugins, HMR). Next: v0.6.0 (Unit Spawner + ECS integration).

---

## Project Structure

### Root Level Files

```
C:\Users\koosh\Dino\
  README.md                 # Feature overview, quick start, architecture diagrams
  CLAUDE.md                 # Agent governance, design rules, legal move classes
  AGENTS.md                 # Subagent capabilities and delegation patterns
  CONTRIBUTING.md           # PR workflow, code style, testing guidelines
  CHANGELOG.md              # Semantic changelog (Keep a Changelog format)
  VERSION                   # Current version (0.5.0)
  llms.txt                  # This file (concise LLM context)
  llms-full.txt             # This file (comprehensive LLM context)
```

### Source Code

```
src/
  DINOForge.sln             # Main solution file

  Runtime/
    DINOForge.Runtime.csproj
    Program.cs              # BepInEx entry point
    Manifest.xml            # BepInEx plugin manifest
    PluginInfo.cs           # Plugin metadata
    Bootstrap.cs            # ECS system injection, world detection
    Debug/
      DebugOverlay.cs       # F10 in-game overlay (Spectre.Console)
      OverlayRenderer.cs    # Render loop integration
    Bridge/
      ComponentMap.cs       # 30+ vanilla-to-mod component mappings
      EntityQueries.cs      # ECS entity query helpers
      StatModifierSystem.cs # Apply mod stat overrides
      VanillaCatalog.cs     # Vanilla component/system registry
      EcsExtensions.cs      # Reflection/introspection utilities
    HotReload/
      PackFileWatcher.cs    # FileSystemWatcher for live reload
      ReloadBridge.cs       # Runtime pack reload triggers
    UI/
      SettingsPanel.cs      # Mod menu UI (netcode/Harmony hooks)
      ModMenuOverlay.cs     # HUD injection

  SDK/
    DINOForge.SDK.csproj
    ContentLoader.cs        # Pack discovery, topological load ordering
    Registry/
      IRegistry.cs          # Generic registry interface
      Registry[T].cs        # Type-safe registry implementation
      RegistryConflict.cs   # Conflict detection & reporting
      PriorityLayering.cs   # Priority (0+, 1000+, 2000+, 3000+)
    Models/
      UnitDefinition.cs     # Unit data model
      BuildingDefinition.cs # Building data model
      FactionArchetype.cs   # Faction (Order, Swarm, Asymmetric)
      WeaponDefinition.cs   # Weapon/attack definitions
      ProjectileDefinition.cs
      DoctrineDefinition.cs # Battle doctrine (focus, tactics, bonuses)
      SkillDefinition.cs    # Unit special abilities
      WaveDefinition.cs     # Wave composition
      SquadDefinition.cs    # Squad archetype
    Validation/
      SchemaValidator.cs    # NJsonSchema integration
      ValidationRule.cs     # Custom validation rules
      ValidatorRegistry.cs  # Rule registry
    Dependencies/
      DependencyResolver.cs # Semver-aware resolution
      ConflictDetector.cs   # Cycle detection, conflict rules
      VersionRange.cs       # Semver parsing/comparison
    Assets/
      AssetsService.cs      # AssetsTools.NET wrapper
      AddressablesCatalog.cs # Addressables catalog reading/writing
      BundleInfo.cs         # Bundle metadata
    HotReload/
      PackWatcher.cs        # FileSystemWatcher implementation
    Universe/
      UniverseBible.cs      # Total conversion system (namespace isolation)
      NamespaceMap.cs       # ID remapping for mods

  Bridge/
    DINOForge.Bridge.csproj
    Protocol/
      GameBridgeMessage.cs  # JSON-RPC message types
      IGameBridge.cs        # Bridge interface
      BridgeRequest.cs      # Request/response types
    Client/
      GameClient.cs         # Out-of-process bridge client
      BridgeConnection.cs   # TCP/IPC connection

  Domains/
    DINOForge.Domains.csproj
    Warfare/
      FactionArchetypeProvider.cs  # Order, Swarm, Asymmetric
      DoctrineLookup.cs            # Doctrine registry + rules
      UnitRoleValidator.cs         # Unit archetype validation
      WaveComposer.cs              # Wave assembly from definitions
      BalanceCalculator.cs         # Stat scaling, progression
      CombatSimulator.cs           # Combat math (experimental)
    Economy/ (planned)
    Scenario/ (planned)
    UI/ (planned)

  Tools/
    Cli/
      DINOForge.Cli.csproj
      Program.cs            # System.CommandLine CLI entry
      Commands/
        StatusCommand.cs    # Status reporting
        QueryCommand.cs     # Registry query
        OverrideCommand.cs  # Live stat override
        ReloadCommand.cs    # Hot reload pack
        WatchCommand.cs     # File watch mode
        SpawnCommand.cs     # Unit spawn (M9)

    PackCompiler/
      DINOForge.PackCompiler.csproj
      Program.cs            # Main CLI
      Commands/
        ValidateCommand.cs  # Pack validation vs schemas
        BuildCommand.cs     # Pack compilation
        AssetCommand.cs     # Asset bundling
      PackBuilder.cs        # Pack assembly logic
      ManifestParser.cs     # pack.yaml parsing

    DumpTools/
      DINOForge.DumpTools.csproj
      Program.cs
      EntityDumper.cs       # Serialize ECS entities → JSON
      ComponentAnalyzer.cs  # Component graph analysis
      ReportGenerator.cs    # Spectre.Console reports

    Installer/
      DINOForge.Installer.csproj
      InstallerService.cs   # BepInEx + DINOForge auto-install
      RegistryUpdater.cs    # NuGet package detection

    McpServer/
      DINOForge.McpServer.csproj
      Program.cs            # MCP server entry
      GameTools/
        UnitQueryTool.cs    # Query units by ID/type
        FactionTool.cs      # List factions, archetypes
        WeaponTool.cs       # Weapon stats, falloff
        BuildingTool.cs     # Building definitions
        StatModifierTool.cs # Stat override queries
        (13 tools total)

  Tests/
    DINOForge.Tests.csproj  # net8.0
    Unit/
      RegistryTests.cs      # Registry conflict, priority, queries
      ContentLoaderTests.cs # Pack loading, ordering, validation
      SchemaValidationTests.cs
      DependencyResolverTests.cs
      AssetServiceTests.cs
      (40+ test files)

    Integration/
      BridgeIntegrationTests.cs
      EndToEndPackLoadTests.cs
      EcsComponentMapTests.cs
      StatModifierTests.cs
      (15+ integration tests)
```

### Content Packs

```
packs/
  example-balance/
    pack.yaml               # Manifest: id, version, type, loads
    units/
      starter-unit.yaml
      elite-unit.yaml
    buildings/
      barracks.yaml

  warfare-modern/
    pack.yaml               # Modern warfare (West vs Enemy)
    factions/
      west-faction.yaml
    units/
      infantry.yaml
      tank.yaml
    doctrines/
      blitzkrieg.yaml

  warfare-starwars/
    pack.yaml               # Clone Wars (Republic vs CIS)
    factions/
      republic.yaml
    units/
      clone-trooper.yaml

  warfare-guerrilla/
    pack.yaml               # Asymmetric warfare
    factions/
      guerrilla.yaml

  economy-balanced/ (planned)
  scenario-tutorial/ (planned)
```

### Schemas (14 JSON files)

```
schemas/
  unit.schema.json          # UnitDefinition shape
  building.schema.json      # BuildingDefinition shape
  faction.schema.json       # FactionArchetype shape
  weapon.schema.json        # WeaponDefinition shape
  projectile.schema.json    # ProjectileDefinition shape
  doctrine.schema.json      # DoctrineDefinition shape
  skill.schema.json         # SkillDefinition shape
  wave.schema.json          # WaveDefinition shape
  squad.schema.json         # SquadDefinition shape
  pack.schema.json          # pack.yaml manifest shape
  stat-modifier.schema.json # Stat override rules
  component-map.schema.json # ECS component mappings
  registry.schema.json      # Generic registry shape
  config.schema.json        # Runtime configuration
```

### Docs & Manifests

```
docs/
  index.md                  # VitePress homepage
  getting-started.md        # Quick start guide
  pack-author-guide.md      # How to write packs
  api-reference.md          # SDK API docs
  architecture.md           # System design deep-dive
  testing-guide.md          # Test writing guide
  tutorial-scenarios.md     # Example scenario packs
  .vitepress/
    config.ts               # VitePress config
    theme.ts                # Custom theme

manifests/
  contracts.yaml            # Public API contracts
  ownership.yaml            # Code ownership map
  extension-points.yaml     # Where agents can extend

.claude/
  commands/
    build-all.sh            # Build + test all
    deploy.sh               # Deploy docs to GitHub Pages
    test.sh                 # Run all tests
    validate.sh             # Validate packs
    dump-analyze.sh         # Run dump analyzer
  memory/
    MEMORY.md               # Agent memory (persists across conversations)
```

---

## Pack System (pack.yaml Schema)

Every mod is a pack with explicit manifest:

```yaml
id: my-pack                          # Unique ID (slug format)
name: My Pack                        # Display name
version: 0.1.0                       # Semver
author: YourName
type: content                        # content | balance | ruleset | total_conversion | utility
framework_version: ">=0.1.0 <1.0.0" # Semver range
depends_on:
  - example-pack@>=0.1.0
conflicts_with:
  - incompatible-pack@*
loads:
  units:
    - units/
    - units/special/
  buildings:
    - buildings/
  factions:
    - factions/
  weapons:
    - weapons/
  doctrines:
    - doctrines/
  skills:
    - skills/
  waves:
    - waves/
  squads:
    - squads/
metadata:
  description: "Pack description"
  tags: ["balance", "modern"]
  min_game_version: "1.0.0"
```

**Load Order**:
1. Discover all `pack.yaml` files
2. Resolve dependencies (topological sort)
3. Detect conflicts (same ID + same type)
4. For each pack in order:
   - Load + validate YAML content against schemas
   - Register entries in type-safe registries
   - Apply stat modifiers
   - Hot-reload if watch mode enabled

---

## CLI Commands

All commands use System.CommandLine and Spectre.Console for rich output.

### PackCompiler

```bash
# Validate pack structure and content against schemas
dotnet run --project src/Tools/PackCompiler -- validate packs/example-balance

# Validate all packs in directory
dotnet run --project src/Tools/PackCompiler -- validate packs/

# Build (compile) a pack into deployable format
dotnet run --project src/Tools/PackCompiler -- build packs/example-balance

# Package assets (bundles, addressables)
dotnet run --project src/Tools/PackCompiler -- asset packs/warfare-modern --output build/
```

### CLI (dinoforge)

Compiled to `src/Tools/Cli/bin/Debug/dinoforge.exe` or installed globally via NuGet.

```bash
# Show mod status
dinoforge status

# Query registry
dinoforge query units --faction west
dinoforge query buildings --type barracks

# Live override (requires game running)
dinoforge override unit cavalry --stat health +50

# Reload packs (hot-reload)
dinoforge reload

# Watch mode (auto-reload on file change)
dinoforge watch packs/

# Spawn unit (M9 feature)
dinoforge spawn cavalry --faction west --tier 2
dinoforge spawn-wave wave-1
```

### DumpTools

```bash
# Dump entities to JSON
dotnet run --project src/Tools/DumpTools -- dump --output entities.json

# Analyze component graph
dotnet run --project src/Tools/DumpTools -- analyze --output report.md

# Filter by component type
dotnet run --project src/Tools/DumpTools -- dump --filter "Health,ArmorData" --output combat-units.json
```

### Custom Commands (.claude/commands/)

```bash
# Build, test, validate all
.claude/commands/build-all.sh

# Run all tests
.claude/commands/test.sh

# Validate all packs
.claude/commands/validate.sh

# Deploy docs
.claude/commands/deploy.sh

# Run analyzer
.claude/commands/dump-analyze.sh
```

---

## ECS Bridge Architecture

### Component Mapping (src/Runtime/Bridge/ComponentMap.cs)

30+ mappings from vanilla DINO components to mod definitions:

| Vanilla Component | Mod Model | Mapping |
|---|---|---|
| Health | UnitDefinition.Health | `health.amount ← definition.maxHealth` |
| ArmorData | UnitDefinition.Armor | `armor.value ← stat_modifiers[armor]` |
| AttackCooldown | WeaponDefinition.Cooldown | `cooldown ← weapon.cooldownFrames` |
| Position3D | UnitDefinition.StartPosition | Position initialization |
| Movement | UnitDefinition.Speed | `speed ← base_speed * (1 + stat_mods)` |
| (27+ more) | | |

**Application**: StatModifierSystem reads registry overrides and applies to entities each frame.

### Entity Queries (src/Runtime/Bridge/EntityQueries.cs)

Helpers for agent introspection:

```csharp
// Get all units of a faction
public static NativeArray<Entity> GetUnitsByFaction(World world, string factionId)

// Get buildings with health < X
public static NativeArray<Entity> GetDamagedBuildings(World world, float threshold)

// Count entities by component type
public static int CountEntitiesWithComponent<T>(World world) where T : unmanaged, IComponentData
```

### Stat Modifier System (src/Runtime/Bridge/StatModifierSystem.cs)

SystemBase that runs each frame:

1. Enumerate all entities with Health, ArmorData, etc.
2. For each entity, query StatModifier registry
3. Apply deltas: `final_stat = base_stat * (1 + additive%) + additive_flat`
4. Clamp to bounds (defined in schema)
5. Log applied modifiers (debug level)

**No monkeypatch — uses ECS ComponentData mutations only.**

### Vanilla Catalog (src/Runtime/Bridge/VanillaCatalog.cs)

Read-only registry of vanilla DINO systems and components:

- System discovery via reflection on `World.Systems` (NoAllocReadOnlyCollection)
- Component introspection via typeof + generic FieldInfo
- **Critical invariant**: World is read during Initialization group, locked during Simulation

---

## Testing Approach

### Test Structure

**Unit Tests** (xUnit + FluentAssertions):
- Registry operations (add, query, conflict detection)
- Schema validation (valid + invalid cases)
- Dependency resolution (cycles, missing deps, version ranges)
- Content loader (pack discovery, ordering, validation)
- Balance calculations (stat scaling formulas)

**Integration Tests**:
- Pack loading end-to-end (file → registry → runtime)
- ECS bridge (component mapping, stat application)
- Asset pipeline (bundle reading/writing)
- CLI commands (validate, build, query, spawn)

**Property-Based Tests** (planned, using Hedgehog or FsCheck):
- Stat calculations remain within bounds over 1000 random inputs
- Dependency resolver always produces acyclic ordering
- Pack load order is deterministic

### Test Run Commands

```bash
# Run all tests
dotnet test src/DINOForge.sln

# Run specific test class
dotnet test src/Tests/DINOForge.Tests.csproj -c TestFixture=RegistryTests

# Run with coverage
dotnet test src/DINOForge.sln /p:CollectCoverage=true /p:CoverageFormat=opencover

# Run in verbose mode
dotnet test src/DINOForge.sln --verbosity detailed
```

### Target Metrics

- **Unit test count**: 130+ (currently 80+)
- **Integration test count**: 25+ (currently 15+)
- **Code coverage**: 75%+ on SDK + Domains
- **Pre-commit gate**: All tests pass + no lint warnings

---

## CI/CD Pipeline

### GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/)

**ci.yml** (on push to main, PR):
- Restore NuGet packages
- Build solution (`dotnet build`)
- Run tests (`dotnet test` with coverage)
- Upload coverage to Codecov
- Lint check (`dotnet format --verify-no-changes`)

**docs.yml** (on release tag):
- Build VitePress site
- Deploy to GitHub Pages

**release.yml** (manual trigger):
- Pack NuGet package (`dotnet pack`)
- Push to nuget.org
- Create GitHub Release with changelog
- Deploy docs

### Release Process

1. Update VERSION file (e.g., `0.6.0`)
2. Update CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format)
3. Commit + tag: `git tag v0.6.0`
4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`
5. GitHub Actions auto-builds, tests, packages, deploys

---

## Key Design Principles

1. **Wrap, Don't Handroll** — Always prefer existing libraries with thin wrappers over custom implementations
2. **Declarative Before Imperative** — YAML/JSON manifests over C# patches
3. **Framework Before Content** — Invest in platform, not individual mods
4. **Stable Abstraction Over Unstable Internals** — Isolate ECS glue behind bridge
5. **Agent-First Repo Design** — Optimize for autonomous agent development
6. **Observability First-Class** — Logs, overlays, reports, validators built-in
7. **Compatibility-Aware Packaging** — Explicit deps, conflicts, versions
8. **Graceful Degradation** — Fail loudly with clear fallbacks

---

## Agent Governance

### MUST DO

- Work through manifests (pack.yaml) and registries (SDK/Registry)
- Use generators/templates for new content packs
- Update CHANGELOG.md when adding features/fixes
- Update README.md if adding new commands or major features
- Add tests for all public APIs
- Run `dotnet test` before completing work
- Validate all packs: `dotnet run --project src/Tools/PackCompiler -- validate packs/`

### MUST NOT

- **Handroll what a library already solves** — always search for existing packages first
- Patch runtime internals unless assigned runtime-layer work
- Invent new registry patterns casually
- Duplicate schemas (consolidate into schemas/ directory)
- Bypass validators (always use SchemaValidator)
- Hardcode content IDs in engine code (always use registries)
- Add undocumented extension points (document in manifests/)
- Skip tests or merge without compatibility checks

### Legal Move Classes (Agent Operations)

Reduce all work to one of these:

- `create schema` — New JSON schema definition (add to schemas/)
- `extend registry` — Add entries to existing registry (modify SDK/Registry)
- `add content pack` — New YAML pack with manifest (add to packs/)
- `patch mapping` — Update vanilla-to-mod mapping (modify Bridge/ComponentMap)
- `write validator` — New validation rule (extend SDK/Validation)
- `add test fixture` — New test case (add to Tests/)
- `add debug view` — New diagnostic surface (extend Runtime/Debug/DebugOverlay)
- `add migration` — Version compatibility migration (add to SDK/Migrations)
- `add compatibility rule` — Cross-pack conflict rule (extend DependencyResolver)
- `add documentation manifest` — Update docs/contracts (manifest-driven)

---

## Code Style

- **C# 12+** with nullable reference types enabled (`#nullable enable`)
- **async/await** preferred over raw Tasks
- **XML doc comments** on all public APIs (methods, classes, interfaces)
- **Immutable data models** preferred (record, init-only properties)
- **Registry pattern** for all extensible domains
- **No `var`** for non-obvious types (explicit typing for clarity)
- **Meaningful names** over comments (self-documenting code)

---

## Build vs Wrap Decision Tree

**ALWAYS prefer** (in order):

1. **Direct use** of existing library as-is (e.g., use NJsonSchema directly)
2. **Thin wrapper/adapter** around existing library (e.g., wrap AssetsTools.NET)
3. **Composition** of multiple existing libraries (e.g., FileSystemWatcher + YamlDotNet)
4. **Modified fork** of existing library (last resort)

**ONLY handroll when**:
- No existing solution covers the need (e.g., DINO-specific ECS glue)
- Wrapping would be more complex than simple implementation
- Scope is tiny and self-contained (< 50 lines)

### Library Preferences

| Need | Use | Wrap/Adapt | Don't Build |
|------|-----|------------|-------------|
| Schema validation | NJsonSchema | Create `SchemaValidator` wrapper | Custom validator |
| Dependency resolution | Semver.NET | Create `DependencyResolver` | Custom semver logic |
| Logging | Serilog + BepInEx | Thin facade in Runtime | Custom logger |
| CLI tooling | System.CommandLine, Spectre.Console | Create command classes | Custom arg parser |
| Config management | BepInEx ConfigurationManager | Wrap in Config service | Custom system |
| ECS introspection | Unity.Entities reflection | Wrap in EntityQueries | Custom reflection |
| File watching | FileSystemWatcher | Use directly | Custom polling |
| YAML/JSON parsing | YamlDotNet, System.Text.Json | Direct use | Custom parsers |
| Asset bundles | AssetsTools.NET | Create `AssetsService` wrapper | Custom unpacker |
| Testing | xUnit, FluentAssertions, Moq | Direct use | Custom framework |

---

## Current Milestone (v0.5.0)

✅ **Complete**:
- Reverse-engineering harness (45K entity dumps)
- Runtime scaffold (BepInEx, ECS systems)
- Generic SDK (registries, schemas, ContentLoader, 46 tests)
- Dev tooling (PackCompiler, DumpTools, debug overlay)
- Warfare domain (archetypes, doctrines, roles, waves, balance, 31 tests)
- ECS bridge (ComponentMap, EntityQueries, StatModifier)
- Asset pipeline (AssetsTools.NET, Addressables)
- GUI installer + NuGet packaging

### Next Milestone (v0.6.0 — M9)

🔄 **In Progress**:
- Unit Spawner System (ECS archetype factory, spawn queue, lifecycle)
- ECS Initialization Pipeline (ordering, stat application, collision-free placement)
- Live Spawn Commands (`dinoforge spawn <unit-id>`)
- Spawner Schema Validation
- Target: 30+ spawner tests, zero frame drops on spawn

---

## DINO Game Details (Reference)

These details inform ECS bridge design. Agents typically don't modify game code directly.

- **Engine**: Unity 2021.3.45f2, full ECS (DOTS), Burst enabled
- **Asset system**: Unity Addressables v1.21.18 (NOT classic AssetBundles)
- **Streaming assets**: `StreamingAssets/aa/StandaloneWindows64/` (4.2GB + 2.1GB catalogs)
- **Mod loader**: BepInEx 5.4.23.5 (standard GitHub build compatible)
- **Runtime**: **Mono** (not IL2CPP) — .NET reflection works
- **MonoBehaviour**: Never runs (destroyed at frame 0) — use ECS SystemBase only
- **World access**: `World.Systems` is `NoAllocReadOnlyCollection` — index access only, NO IEnumerable cast
- **Entity count**: ~45K in Default World, 6 worlds total
- **Faction system**: No explicit Faction component — implicit via Enemy tag + unit type markers
- **Namespaces**: Game code in `Components.*`, `Systems.*` from DNO.Main.dll
- **Key components**: Health, ArmorData, AttackCooldown, BuildingBase, ProjectileDataBase
- **System groups**: Initialization → Simulation → PathFinding → Fight → ResourceDelivery → ResourceProducing → Presentation

---

- **GitHub Repo**: https://github.com/KooshaPari/Dino
- **Full Documentation**: https://kooshapari.github.io/Dino

- **BepInEx Docs**: https://bepinex.github.io/
- **Unity DOTS**: https://unity.com/dots
- **AssetsTools.NET**: https://github.com/nesrak1/AssetsTools.NET
- **NJsonSchema**: https://github.com/RicoSuter/NJsonSchema
- **Keep a Changelog**: https://keepachangelog.com/
- **Semantic Versioning**: https://semver.org/
- **llmstxt.org**: https://llmstxt.org/

---

## Quick Reference: Common Tasks

### Add a new pack type

1. Define schema in `schemas/my-type.schema.json`
2. Create model class in `SDK/Models/MyTypeDefinition.cs`
3. Create registry in `SDK/Registry/MyTypeRegistry.cs` (extends IRegistry[T])
4. Add validator in `SDK/Validation/MyTypeValidator.cs`
5. Update `ContentLoader.cs` to load new pack type
6. Add 3+ unit tests in `Tests/Unit/`
7. Add integration test in `Tests/Integration/`
8. Update CHANGELOG.md, README.md
9. Run `dotnet test` (must pass 100%)

### Fix a bug

1. Create minimal failing test in `Tests/`
2. Fix code until test passes
3. Run full test suite: `dotnet test`
4. Update CHANGELOG.md (under `## [Unreleased] - Fixes`)
5. Commit with message: `fix: <description>`

### Add a CLI command

1. Create command class in `src/Tools/Cli/Commands/MyCommand.cs`
2. Register in `Program.cs` (System.CommandLine)
3. Add 2+ unit tests in `Tests/Unit/CliTests/`
4. Add integration test (if interacts with game)
5. Update docs/cli-reference.md
6. Run `dotnet test && dinoforge <command> --help`

### Deploy docs

```bash
.claude/commands/deploy.sh
# Builds VitePress, pushes to gh-pages branch, triggers GitHub Pages rebuild
```

---

## Glossary

- **Pack**: A mod, consisting of pack.yaml manifest + content files
- **Registry**: Type-safe collection of game objects (units, buildings, etc.)
- **ECS Bridge**: Maps mod definitions to vanilla DINO ECS components at runtime
- **StatModifier**: Runtime override of entity stats (health, armor, speed, etc.)
- **ComponentMap**: 30+ mappings from vanilla components to mod data models
- **Hot Reload**: Live reloading of pack files during gameplay (via FileSystemWatcher)
- **Addressables**: Unity's runtime asset loading system (used for bundles)
- **AssetsTools.NET**: Community tool for reading/writing .assets and .bundle files
- **BepInEx**: Mod loader framework, injects into game at startup
- **DOTS**: Unity Data-Oriented Tech Stack (ECS architecture)
- **SDD**: Spec-Driven Development (specs/schemas first, implementation second)
- **BDD**: Behavior-Driven Development (user stories/acceptance criteria first)
- **TDD**: Test-Driven Development (tests first, code second)
- **DDD**: Domain-Driven Design (bounded contexts, ubiquitous language)
- **ADD**: Agent-Driven Development (autonomous agent code generation)
- **CDD**: Contract-Driven Development (schemas as contracts)
